title: The Therapists

jokes.text: Honestly, you don't look like that guy anymore.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Larry being a changed man
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's blunt assessment that his appearance confirms the change
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry David dynamic - Cheryl's matter-of-fact delivery about his previous slovenliness

jokes.text: You look like a real person, not an eighth-grader with silly shoes.
jokes.setup: Larry's new tucked-in appearance
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to an eighth-grader with silly shoes
jokes.explanation: Perfect specificity - 'eighth-grader with silly shoes' is wonderfully precise and visual

jokes.text: I used to walk around in sneakers like an eighth-grader.
jokes.setup: Cheryl's eighth-grader comparison
jokes.punchline: Larry accepting and reinforcing the comparison
jokes.explanation: Quick callback that reinforces the visual comedy of Larry's previous appearance

jokes.text: I can't see underwater 'cause, you know... But whatever, I'll feel around for the fish, you know.
jokes.setup: Larry promising to go scuba diving despite his limitations
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of him blindly feeling around underwater for fish
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist image that perfectly captures Larry's desperate attempts to change while highlighting his limitations

jokes.text: The only vestige of my old self is that I've still got the good breath, you know.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming to be completely changed
jokes.punchline: The one thing he's keeping from his old self is good breath
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Larry logic - of all things to keep, he chooses breath. Shows his warped priorities beautifully

jokes.text: That's the thing you're keeping?
jokes.setup: Larry's declaration about keeping his good breath
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's incredulous reaction highlighting the absurdity
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction that amplifies the absurdity of Larry's priority system

jokes.text: Let me just talk to my therapist and see what she has to say first.
jokes.setup: Larry asking Cheryl on a date, expecting acceptance
jokes.punchline: Cheryl needs therapist approval for a simple date
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup for the episode's main premise - the therapist dependency that will drive all future complications

jokes.text: Don't they make too much money? I mean, everybody buys a drink. They get, what, 20% of every drink? That's insane, isn't it?
jokes.setup: Simple question about tipping the bartender
jokes.punchline: Larry's convoluted math and outrage about bartender income
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry - takes a simple social interaction and finds mathematical outrage in it

jokes.text: They're on their feet all night, so whatever. Whatever they make-- good for them. They work hard, you know.
jokes.setup: Larry's rant about bartenders making too much
jokes.punchline: Immediate complete reversal to supporting them
jokes.explanation: Shows 'new Larry' trying to be supportive despite his natural instincts

jokes.text: I have to say, it's not surprising, because I'd like to think I know what I'm doing.
jokes.setup: Larry's success with Cheryl
jokes.punchline: Therapist's smug self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Sets up the therapist's overconfidence that will lead to disaster

jokes.text: Sometimes it works out you go to a dinner and a movie, sometimes it's better to have dinner after the movie, so you have something to talk about.
jokes.setup: Planning the date logistics
jokes.punchline: Larry's overthinking of basic date sequencing
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry over-analysis of social conventions

jokes.text: Let's not even waste time talking about that. Movie, dinner.
jokes.setup: Larry's elaborate dinner/movie analysis
jokes.punchline: Therapist's curt dismissal and definitive ruling
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Larry's neurotic planning and the therapist's authoritative dismissal

jokes.text: She has until Monday to decide, or that's it.
jokes.setup: Romantic advice about telling Cheryl he loves her
jokes.punchline: Sudden ultimatum strategy
jokes.explanation: Sets up the major plot device - terrible romantic advice that will backfire spectacularly

jokes.text: She might not like it at first, but there's one thing she won't be able to say, is that the guy who just said that is a pussy.
jokes.setup: Defending the ultimatum strategy
jokes.punchline: Crude reasoning about not appearing weak
jokes.explanation: Shows the therapist's misguided masculine posturing that will doom Larry

jokes.text: What, you're getting a lot of pussy from me? Not an amount that is not manageable.
jokes.setup: Discussion about being perceived as weak
jokes.punchline: Larry's awkward attempt to discuss his sex life
jokes.explanation: Larry's uncomfortable oversharing with clinical phrasing is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Although I gotta say, being a pussy really wasn't my problem with her. It was more the fact that I was selfish and kind of thoughtless...
jokes.setup: Therapist's theory about Larry's relationship problems
jokes.punchline: Larry's accurate self-assessment of his actual flaws
jokes.explanation: Moment of genuine self-awareness from Larry that makes his following the bad advice even funnier

jokes.text: We have to stop.
jokes.setup: Larry trying to continue the conversation about his real problems
jokes.punchline: Abrupt session termination right when Larry shows insight
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing - cuts off just when Larry shows real self-awareness, ensuring he follows the bad advice

jokes.text: Je t'aime.
jokes.setup: End of therapy session
jokes.punchline: Therapist randomly speaking French
jokes.explanation: Random pretentious flourish that adds to the therapist's questionable character

jokes.text: Not just happy, but really, like, delirious.
jokes.setup: Larry promising to make Cheryl happy
jokes.punchline: Escalating to 'delirious' happiness
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to over-promise with increasingly dramatic language

jokes.text: He runs around the house all day naked, chasing himself, playing tag.
jokes.setup: Describing his father-in-law's Alzheimer's symptoms
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of naked self-tag
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd and specific visual that's both funny and slightly disturbing

jokes.text: There you go-- 25 bucks.
jokes.setup: Funkhouser asking for $250 sponsorship
jokes.punchline: Larry only gives $25, one-tenth the amount
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry cheapness despite the heartfelt setup about Alzheimer's

jokes.text: I know, but I feel like usually you would complain or, I don't know, give him a hard time, but...
jokes.setup: Larry being unusually agreeable about the donation
jokes.punchline: Cheryl noting how his normal behavior would be to complain
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Larry's usual argumentative nature

jokes.text: I want you to move back in with me. You have till Monday to decide.
jokes.setup: Romantic dinner conversation going well
jokes.punchline: Sudden jarring ultimatum
jokes.explanation: Perfect payoff to the therapist's terrible advice - completely destroys the romantic moment

jokes.text: You're giving me an ultimatum?
jokes.setup: Larry's romantic ultimatum
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's incredulous reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect straight-man reaction that highlights the absurdity of the ultimatum in a romantic context

jokes.text: Okay, there's no ultimatum. Take your time. Take a month, I don't care.
jokes.setup: Cheryl's immediate rejection
jokes.punchline: Larry's desperate backpedaling
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry panic response - complete reversal of his confident ultimatum

jokes.text: Cheryl, the therapist told me to say that.
jokes.setup: Cheryl walking away after the ultimatum disaster
jokes.punchline: Larry throwing his therapist under the bus
jokes.explanation: Larry's desperate attempt to salvage the situation by betraying his therapist

jokes.text: You know what, Larry? I thought the new Larry didn't lie.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming the therapist made him do it
jokes.punchline: Cheryl calling out the contradiction with 'new Larry'
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that exposes the contradiction in Larry's 'changed man' persona

jokes.text: I'm so stupid. I was actually believing you.
jokes.setup: Larry's explanations falling apart
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's self-recrimination for believing him
jokes.explanation: Cheryl's genuine hurt at being fooled again adds emotional weight to the comedy

jokes.text: You ruined my life. You ruined my life, okay?
jokes.setup: The date disaster
jokes.punchline: Larry's dramatic overstatement of consequences
jokes.explanation: Larry's tendency to catastrophize and blame others for his problems

jokes.text: I had to see someone else who was suicidal.
jokes.setup: Larry blaming the therapist for cutting off the session
jokes.punchline: Therapist's casual mention of suicidal patient
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mental health priorities and the therapist's casual attitude

jokes.text: You get the face you deserve, right?
jokes.setup: Therapist explaining why appearance matters for advice
jokes.punchline: Pseudo-philosophical justification for shallow judgment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the therapist's pretentious attempts to justify his shallow thinking

jokes.text: Why are you taking credit for new Larry?
jokes.setup: Therapist claiming responsibility for Larry's changes
jokes.punchline: Larry's indignation at losing credit for his own improvement
jokes.explanation: Larry's ego can't even let the therapist take credit for his positive changes

jokes.text: It's not an exact science.
jokes.setup: Larry calling him a moron for the ultimatum advice
jokes.punchline: Massive understatement about therapy being inexact
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response that minimizes his catastrophic advice failure

jokes.text: You look like a rapist with that thing on. You don't need that.
jokes.setup: Larry putting on a hood for the fake mugging
jokes.punchline: Therapist's blunt visual assessment
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about appearance and criminal stereotypes

jokes.text: Isn't that a cliche?
jokes.setup: Being told to put the hood up for mugging
jokes.punchline: Larry's concern about using tired criminal tropes
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about crime clichés while in the middle of planning a fake crime

jokes.text: Give me your bag. I'm mugging you.
jokes.setup: The fake mugging plan
jokes.punchline: Therapist's polite, formal mugging declaration
jokes.explanation: The polite, matter-of-fact way he announces the mugging is absurdly formal

jokes.text: Very funny. Spread your legs.
jokes.setup: Larry giving his name as Larry David to the therapist
jokes.punchline: She thinks he's joking and assumes he's a criminal
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection - the real Larry David name sounds fake, leading to his arrest

jokes.text: The only thing I did think about for a second was my glasses, but that's it-- only the safety of my glasses. 'Cause these are irreplaceable.
jokes.setup: Larry claiming he didn't think twice about being heroic
jokes.punchline: His only concern was for his glasses
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry priorities - even in his heroic moment, he's worried about material concerns

jokes.text: You know, I have been doing that most of my life. You know, I gave a kidney to my friend...
jokes.setup: Larry claiming to be a lifelong hero
jokes.punchline: Casually mentioning kidney donation
jokes.explanation: Good callback to Larry's genuine heroic act, used to build his fake heroic persona

jokes.text: My feet stick out through the fucking bars.
jokes.setup: Describing his prison cell
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of feet sticking through bars
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of prison discomfort with specific absurd detail

jokes.text: You're not even that tall. How tall are you? You're 5'10"? Stand up. Let me see. You're not 5'10".
jokes.setup: Therapist complaining about cell being too small
jokes.punchline: Larry questioning his height claims instead of showing sympathy
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry behavior - getting distracted by height accuracy while his therapist is in jail

jokes.text: In the heel, I am-- that's not the point.
jokes.setup: Larry questioning his height
jokes.punchline: Therapist defending his height measurement including shoes
jokes.explanation: Shows how Larry's tangent has derailed the therapist from his prison complaints

jokes.text: You got a deck of cards? Can you play solitaire or anything?
jokes.setup: Therapist desperate to get out of prison
jokes.punchline: Larry suggesting prison entertainment options
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete insensitivity to the therapist's urgent situation

jokes.text: What are you talking about? Just get me out of fucking prison. I'm not talking about cards.
jokes.setup: Larry's casual prison entertainment suggestions
jokes.punchline: Therapist's frustrated reality check
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Larry's casual attitude and the therapist's desperation

jokes.text: That's why she told Cheryl not to see me. She wants me for herself.
jokes.setup: Cheryl's therapist advising against seeing Larry
jokes.punchline: Larry's narcissistic interpretation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Larry narcissism - turning professional advice against him into evidence of attraction

jokes.text: I raised money. I don't walk.
jokes.setup: Larry seeing Funkhouser at coffee instead of walking
jokes.punchline: Casual admission he doesn't actually walk in the walkathon
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal - the walkathon organizer doesn't walk, just raises money

jokes.text: You were diagnosed with Alzheimer's?!
jokes.setup: Funkhouser talking about Alzheimer's fundraising
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked realization Funkhouser has it
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection - Larry assumes it's about the father-in-law, but it's Funkhouser himself

jokes.text: I ordered chicken salad? I don't even like chicken salad.
jokes.setup: Being told he ordered chicken salad
jokes.punchline: Memory loss evidence - he doesn't remember or like what he ordered
jokes.explanation: Subtle but effective demonstration of memory loss symptoms

jokes.text: I-- I don't remember.
jokes.setup: Police asking Larry to describe the mugger
jokes.punchline: Larry claiming not to remember his own therapist
jokes.explanation: Larry's panic at having to identify his own accomplice

jokes.text: I think it's going to work out. You did it. That was-- There you go, big winner.
jokes.setup: Therapist clearly depressed about his situation
jokes.punchline: Larry's completely oblivious celebration of success
jokes.explanation: Larry's complete lack of awareness about the therapist's mental state

jokes.text: Um, well, one of my clients is this bald guy, um...
jokes.setup: Dr. Salvin seeking therapy about her bizarre experience
jokes.punchline: Her description of Larry as 'this bald guy' to another therapist
jokes.explanation: Perfect circular ending - therapist needing therapy about Larry, reducing him to 'bald guy'

jokes.text: I love you, Larry.
jokes.setup: The entire episode of Larry trying to win Cheryl back
jokes.punchline: She says she loves him right after hearing about his deception
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic ending - Larry gets what he wanted through complete dishonesty, not his 'new Larry' persona

tmdb_overview: Larry tries to use Cheryl's therapist to get back into her good graces after getting bad advice from his own; Larry's miffed at Marty's participation level in an Alzheimer's walk
